**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Temperature for Hf rate reduction factor: 1200°C
*   Hf reduces scale growth rate by a factor of 40 (at 1200°C)

*   Sulfur level: ~1 ppm
*   Desulfurization of Rene N5 to 0.7 ppma S. For desulfurized Re N5 it is 0.9 ppma S.
* EB-PVD process at ~1000°C.

*   **Figure 2b:** Phase transformations in (b) Ni-16Cr-11Al-Y bond coat on a superalloy
    *   X axis: Time, h

*   Figure 3: A trace of the bond coat/TGO interface
    *   Components: Interface Trace, Y' domain
    *   Scale bar: 20 micro-m
    *   Arrows: designate the base of all large TGO instabilities

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   950°C: Temperature for phase transformations on superalloy substrates
*   1150°C: Temperature for thermal cycling of bond coats where martensite transformation was observed
*   -2%: Volume change for the Beta to martensite transformation
*   1100°C: Temperature at which alloys with martensite + Y' structure revert to Beta phase.
*   f = 0.76: Fraction of life for the bond coat/TGO interface in Figure 3
*   70%: Percentage of instabilities with the Beta-phase located at the base
*   75%: Probability of Y' being present on at least one side of instability
*   Al content of (36-34%) and (28-22%)

    *   **Key Values:**
        *   Thermal conductivity (λ) = 1.0 W/m-K (inner layer)
        *   Thermal conductivity (λ) = 2.2 W/m-K (outer layer)

**3. Tables:**
NONE

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 7:**
    *   **Components:** Two-layer coating structure consisting an inner layer and outer layer.
    *   **Labels:** Showing the thermal conductivity value for each layer.

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   Thermal conductivity of Gd, Nd, Yb doped EB-PVD at 500°C: 0.88, 1.00, 1.02 W/m-K (respectively)
*   Coating thickness: 150 µm
*   4 mol% dopant addition
*   Thermal conductivity of EB-PVD reference YSZ: ~1.6 W/m-K
*   Ceramic/metal interface temperature: 950-1100°C
*   APS YSZ (8 wt% or 4.55 mol% Y2O3) thermal conductivity initial: ~1 W/m-K
*    APS YSZ (8 wt% or 4.55 mol% Y2O3) thermal conductivity after 20h test: ~1.4 W/m-K
*   ZrO2-Y2O3-Nd2O3-Yb2O3 and ZrO2-Y2O3-Gd2O3-Yb2O3 thermal conductivity: ~0.6 W/m-K
*   Dopant level: 6-13 mol%
*    EB-PVD ZrO2-(4-6 mol%)Y2O3-Nd2O3-Yb2O3 thermal conductivity: 0.85 W/m-K.
*   EB-PVD YSZ (8 wt% Y2O3) thermal conductivity: 1.85-1.9 W/m-K (after 20-h test)
*   Coating thickness for early deposition: <100 µm
*   Early deposition thermal conductivity: 0.8-1 W/m-K
*   Coating thickness: 250 µm
*   Thermal conductivity: 1.5-1.9 W/m-K (at room temperature)
*   Inner fine-structured zone: 100 µm
*   Bulk YSZ thermal conductivity: 2.2 W/m-K
*   Inner fine-structured grain size: 3-4 µm
*   Layer thickness: < 100 µm
*   Thermal conductivity reduction by layering: 37-45%
*   APSYSZ Y2O3 (8 wt.% Y2O3) thermal conductivity initial: 1.0 W/m-K
*    APSYSZ Y2O3 (8 wt.% Y2O3) thermal conductivity after 30h at 990°C: 1.15 W/m-K
*   APSYSZ Y2O3 (8 wt.% Y2O3) thermal conductivity after 3

        *   Left Y-axis: MOLE FRACTION OF PHASES, values from 0.00 to 1.00
        *   Right Y-axis: MOLE PERCENT YTTRIA IN NONTRANSFORMABLE TETRAGONAL (T'), values from 0 to 12

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   4-20 mol% Gd2O3
*   ZrO2-4 mol% Y2O3 : Onset of measurable shrinkage ~1100°C
*   ZrO2-4 mol% Gd2O3 : Onset of measurable shrinkage ~1175°C
*   8 mol% Gd2O3:  Maximum grain size and shrinkage
*   8.6 mol% Y2O3 in Plasma-sprayed YSZ
*   Below ~1200°C:  Metastable, non-transformable t' phase
*   EB-PVD YSZ (7 wt.% Y2O3)
*   Temperature range 1200 - 1400°C
*   6-8 wt.% EB-PVD YSZ
*   4 wt.% Y2O3 in t phase
*   16 wt.% Y2O3 in c phase
*   ~1000°C: Deposition temperature
*   10 h at 1400°C and 30 h at 1300°C
*   ~20 mol% m phase after 100 h at 1400°C
*   1200 - 1400°C
*   ~55 mol% c phase at 1400°C
*   100 h
*   1150°C: EB-PVD TBCs stable up to 100 h
*   1400°C: Transformation to t+c+m phases after annealing for 100 h
*   1371°C: After 100h, from < 10 mol% to > 30 mol% m phase with slow furnace cooling
*   Yttria content of t' phase lowered to ~5 mol% after 100 h at 1400°C
